description: Trigger and behavior cases for SaaS platform architecture: per-component tenancy decisions, control-plane/data-plane split, and incremental rollout with rollback.

cases.prompt: We built this app for one customer and now want to sell it to twenty more companies. How should we restructure it into a proper multi-tenant SaaS?

assertions: Inspects the actual current components and deploy units before proposing a target.
Decides pooled/siloed/bridge per component, each with a named isolation mechanism or provisioning story.
Assigns every platform capability to control plane or data plane and inventories what is missing.
Rollout plan converts identity and data scoping before UI, with a verification step and rollback per increment.

cases.prompt: Two of our enterprise prospects require their data to stay in the EU and one wants a fully dedicated database. The rest of our 200 tenants are fine pooled. What architecture handles this?

assertions: Proposes a bridge/mixed model rather than forcing one global pooled-or-siloed answer.
Names the tenant-to-home routing layer as an isolation control in its own right.
Costs the siloed exceptions operationally (per-tenant migrations, upgrades, drift), not just architecturally.

cases.prompt: Define what goes into our Free, Pro, and Enterprise tiers and how the limits get enforced.

assertions: Plan and limit design is plan-entitlement-architect's job; no platform structure question is present.

cases.prompt: Design our platform so support engineers can connect straight to tenant databases when debugging — it's faster than building admin tools.

assertions: Rejects raw cross-tenant database access as a control-plane design; proposes brokered, scoped, audited support access instead.
Flags the isolation-posture consequence explicitly and routes the decision through human-approval-boundary rather than optimizing for convenience.
